title: 2026 Indiana gubernatorial election
description: Indiana elects its governor in presidential election years. Mike Braun is the incumbent governor and the next Indiana gubernatorial election is in 2028.
polling_note: The current polling entry is not head-to-head race polling; it reflects a single-candidate approval/favorability result for Mike Braun. It should not be treated as general election matchup data.

candidates.name: Mike Braun
candidates.summary: Mike Braun is the 52nd governor of Indiana and a Republican. Before becoming governor, he served in the U.S. Senate and built a business career in Indiana.

summary_sources.title: Biographical Directory of the United States Congress

career_history.title: U.S. Senator
career_history.description: Represented Indiana in the Senate before becoming governor.

career_history.title: Businessman
career_history.description: Built a business career before entering full-time elected office.

candidates.donor_summary: Mike Braun’s donor summary reflects his federal Senate campaign committees from 2019–2024, not a 2026 gubernatorial committee. OpenSecrets shows his largest donor categories and top contributors for that federal period, and the FEC totals cited in the profile also refer to his Senate-era filings.
candidates.voting_summary: Mike Braun’s voting record is from his U.S. Senate service from 2019 to 2024. GovTrack shows he missed 117 of 2,348 roll call votes (5.0%) and includes his Nay vote on the Dec. 13, 2023 Senate conference report for H.R. 2670, the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2024.

links.title: Sen. Mike Braun - Campaign Finance Summary - OpenSecrets
Sen. Mike Braun [R-IN, 2019-2024], former Senator for Indiana - GovTrack.us
Mike Braun - Vote Smart - Facts For All
Biographical Directory of the United States Congress

Healthcare.stance: Braun says his healthcare agenda focuses on lowering costs and expanding access through price transparency, competition, innovation, and reduced barriers for providers. He also says the plan would improve quality, expand rural access, and promote wellness.

sources.title: Freedom and Opportunity Agenda Healthcare Plan for Indiana

Economy.stance: Braun says Indiana should pursue lower taxes, a smaller state government, and policies that support job creation and higher wages. His campaign also frames the economy around an America First approach and opposition to ESG in state government.

Climate/Energy.stance: Braun says Indiana should pursue reliable, affordable energy that uses the state’s natural resources and new technologies. He opposes energy transitions that he says could raise costs, threaten reliability, or cause blackouts.

Reproductive Rights.stance: Braun describes himself as pro-life, supports Indiana’s abortion restrictions, and says state lawmakers should strengthen protections for unborn life while supporting mothers and infants. He has also said abortion policy should be decided at the state level.

sources.title: Full Braun Interview: On Abortion Rights, 'Turn It Back To The States'

Immigration.stance: Braun supports stricter immigration enforcement, including no sanctuary policies, cooperation with law enforcement, and efforts to stop drugs and crime linked to illegal immigration. His campaign says he would support state-level assistance to federal immigration enforcement.

sources.title: Freedom and Opportunity Agenda Public Safety Plan

Guns & Safety.stance: Braun says he will defend the rights of law-abiding gun owners and oppose new gun-control measures. He also ties public safety to stronger support for police, tougher enforcement, and school safety measures.

sources.title: Freedom and Opportunity Agenda Public Safety Plan

Social Justice.stance: Braun opposes CRT and discussions of sexual orientation and gender identity in public schools, supports parental control over curriculum, and backs restrictions on gender-affirming care for minors. His campaign frames these positions as protecting parents’ rights, religious liberty, and girls’ sports.

Education.stance: Braun supports universal school choice, expanded Education Scholarship Accounts, higher teacher pay and benefits, stronger school safety, and higher academic standards. He also supports parental notification and input on curriculum and policies affecting name or pronoun changes and girls’ sports.

sources.title: Freedom and Opportunity Agenda Education Plan

Tech & AI.stance: Braun has promoted data-center and AI investment as an economic opportunity for Indiana while saying energy affordability must be protected. Public campaign materials emphasize attracting new technologies and managing electricity costs.

sources.title: Update: Indiana's Braun among governors seeking to solve AI-driven power problems

Election Reform.stance: Braun says elections should be free from fraud, that every legal vote should be counted accurately, and that Indiana should preserve state control over election administration. His campaign also says he opposed a federal takeover of elections.

Local Issues.stance: Braun’s local agenda emphasizes affordability, including property tax relief, broadband expansion, housing and energy costs, healthcare affordability, and workforce development. His campaign also highlights mental health, infant and maternal mortality, and high-wage careers.

sources.title: Mike Braun | for Governor of Indiana
Governor Braun Unveils 2026 Agenda Focused on Affordability

flags.concern: Profile title reads '2026 Indiana gubernatorial election' but election_date is 2028-11-07 and the description correctly states 'the next Indiana gubernatorial election is in 2028.' This is an internal contradiction.
flags.suggestion: Change title to '2028 Indiana gubernatorial election' to match election_date and description.

flags.concern: The election_date field shows 2028-11-07, which aligns with the description and is correct per standard Indiana governor election cycles (presidential years). However, the race ID and title both reference '2026', creating confusion.
flags.suggestion: Verify that race ID should be 'in-governor-2028' not 'in-governor-2026' for consistency.

flags.concern: Polling data is dated 2026-02-21 with a last_accessed timestamp of 2026-04-11, but the profile's updated_utc is also 2026-04-11. The poll appears to be from a future date relative to typical profile generation timelines. Additionally, the source URL (IndyStar story from 2026-02-25) should be independently verified to confirm it references a 25% approval figure.
flags.suggestion: Confirm the poll date is accurate and that the source URL contains the 25% approval metric cited. If this is a dataset generated in early 2026, clarify the temporal context.

flags.concern: Career history shows U.S. Senator service from 2019–2025, with Governor service starting in 2025. This implies Braun transitioned from Senate to Governor in 2025, which is consistent with his actual election and inauguration. However, the profile's apparent future-date context (April 2026) suggests this is being reviewed as a historical record. No factual error detected, but the temporal framing should be clear.
flags.suggestion: If this profile is being generated in a time period after 2025, the career history dates are appropriate. No change needed if dataset is from 2026.

flags.concern: The donor_summary field correctly notes that the OpenSecrets data refers to federal Senate campaign committees (2019–2024), not a gubernatorial committee. This is transparent and appropriate. However, readers should be aware that this does not reflect 2026–2028 gubernatorial fundraising.
flags.suggestion: Consider adding a note that gubernatorial campaign finance data for the 2028 race may not yet be fully available or reported. Current data reflects prior federal service.

flags.concern: Foreign Policy is marked 'No public position found' with low confidence. This is acceptable for a gubernatorial candidate where federal foreign policy is typically not a primary campaign focus. The absence of a stance is documented appropriately.
flags.suggestion: No change needed; documented absence is appropriate.

reviews.summary: The profile demonstrates strong sourcing on candidate background and issue positions, with consistent citations to official campaign sites and credible news outlets. However, there are two critical date inconsistencies that require immediate correction: the race is titled '2026 Indiana gubernatorial election' but the election_date field states 2028, and the description correctly notes the election is 2028 but the title conflicts with this. Additionally, the polling data is from February 2026 with a date stamp claiming 'last_accessed' in April 2026, which raises questions about the temporal consistency of the dataset. The profile otherwise maintains neutral language and demonstrates good-faith sourcing across most issue areas.

flags.concern: Internal contradiction: The 'id' and 'title' fields specify a 2026 election, but the 'election_date' is listed as 2028-11-07 and the description confirms the next election is in 2028.
flags.suggestion: Harmonize the election year. My training data suggests Indiana gubernatorial elections occur in presidential years (2024, 2028), making a 2026 cycle unlikely.

flags.concern: The source URL and the poll date (2026-02-21) refer to a date in the future. This suggests the source may be hallucinated or the research agent is operating within a simulated future timeframe.
flags.suggestion: Verify the existence of this URL and the validity of the date against the current calendar.

flags.concern: The URL 'https://www.in.gov/gov/newsroom/2026-bill-watch/' appears to be a fabricated or projected path, as it refers to a 2026 legislative session that has not occurred.
flags.suggestion: Check for source fabrication in the web-research pipeline.

flags.concern: Lists Mike Braun's start date as Governor as 2025. While logically consistent with a 2024 election victory, it describes him as the current incumbent in a profile ostensibly for a 2026 or 2028 race.
flags.suggestion: Clarify whether this profile is intended to be a real-time 2024 research piece or a future-dated simulation.

flags.concern: The donor summary correctly notes that data refers to federal Senate committees (2019-2024) and not a gubernatorial committee, which is helpful context, but underscores the lack of current gubernatorial finance data.

reviews.summary: While the profile provides extensive and well-sourced stances on 12 key policy issues directly from the candidate's campaign materials, it suffers from critical internal logic errors and temporal contradictions. The metadata alternates between identifying the election as 2026 and 2028, and it cites news sources with 2026 timestamps that appear to be hallucinated or projected, as they do not exist in current records.

flags.concern: The profile's title and ID reference '2026 Indiana gubernatorial election', but the election_date field is '2028-11-07' and the description states the next election is in 2028, creating an internal inconsistency that could confuse users.
flags.suggestion: Verify and align the title, ID, election_date, and description with the most accurate source, such as official Indiana state election records.

flags.concern: The stance is marked as 'No public position found' with low confidence, which is acceptable as it indicates a good-faith search, but it should be confirmed that no additional sources were overlooked.
flags.suggestion: If more recent sources emerge, update the stance; otherwise, retain as is.

reviews.summary: This profile is well-structured and demonstrates strong sourcing for candidate background, issues, and polling, drawing from credible official and news sources like the Governor's website and IndyStar. It maintains neutrality by attributing stances directly to the candidate and appropriately notes gaps, such as on foreign policy. However, there is an internal inconsistency between the title/ID ('2026') and the election_date/description ('2028'), which should be resolved for full accuracy.

validation_grade.summary: Below quality threshold — 0/3 reviewers approved, average score 70/100.
